call_sites_changed: engine/split/execute.go ExecuteSplitRedirectStub: now acquires cs.LockFileContent(originalFileID) before the stub content write, holds it through cat.Put + InvalidateHeaderCache via defer

engine/split/execute.go ExecuteSplitAtomic: now acquires cs.LockFileContent(originalFileID) before the stub content write, releases it explicitly right after InvalidateHeaderCache (before B+Tree inserts / graph edges), with a lockHeld-guarded defer covering early-return error paths

lock_ordering_analysis.existing_order_in_Append: cs.stripes[stripeFor(fileID)] -> wal.Writer-internal (via wal.AppendAndApply) -> cat.stripes[stripeFor(fileID)] (via cat.Put, nested inside the AppendAndApply closure)
lock_ordering_analysis.FileGuard_interaction: none -- FileGuard is a separate, independent CAS map with no shared state with cs.stripes/cat.stripes/wal.Writer; acquired by the caller before either split-commit function runs, released after (guard.Release), entirely outside this lock's scope
lock_ordering_analysis.risk_of_new_deadlock: none identified -- no new lock pairs introduced beyond the ones Append already exercises under the existing catalog/content_test.go and catalog_test.go concurrency stress tests
lock_ordering_analysis.verification_adversarial_check_2_prior_r: PASS (030-verification's own adversarial check 2 already confirmed no lock-order-inversion/deadlock existed in the pre-fix code around headerCacheMu/cs.stripes/InvalidateHeaderCache; this fix does not touch headerCacheMu's own locking, only adds cs.stripes acquisition around call sites that previously took no ContentStore-level lock at all)

regression_surface: engine/split/execute_test.go's existing TestSectionIndexInvalidation, TestSplitAtomicCommit, TestExecuteSplitRedirectStub*, and all other ExecuteSplitAtomic/ExecuteSplitRedirectStub callers -- verified via full ./split/... -race suite (see self-consistency.json)
engine/catalog/... -race suite (unaffected by this fix's LockFileContent addition, but re-run in full per instructions since it's the package the new exported method lives in)
